The more countries Iâve traveled to, the more certain Iâve become. Taiwan holds landscapes that truly stand on a world-class level.

Kaiweng Canyon has always been on my bucket list. But seeing it with your own eyes is never easy. 

Itâs remote, and the steep descent alone is enough to make you think twice about coming back.

I didnât make it all the way down to some of its most iconic spots this time, but capturing it from above was already breathtaking.

Planning your year around the wild? ð Here are 12 unforgettable national parks, one for every month, with the best time to visit and what makes each one special

ð January â Ras Muhammad, Egypt (@jaumemallart): Dive into the Red Seaâs vibrant coral reefs and desert meets sea landscapes. Best weather for snorkeling and diving.

ðï¸ February â LenÃ§Ã³is Maranhenses, Brazil (@leocastrofilms): Endless white sand dunes dotted with freshwater lagoons after the rains. One of Brazilâs surreal natural wonders.

ð´ March â Daintree, Australia (@backcountryblissadventures): The worldâs oldest rainforest at its lushest. Spot cassowaries, crocs, and ancient plant species.

ð¿ April â Khao Sok, Thailand (@florisgone): Rainforest-covered limestone cliffs and floating bungalows on Cheow Lan Lake. April is dry, but green.

ð May â Jasper, Canada (@yakeandmarie): Glacier-fed lakes start to thaw, wildlife is active, and the trails are clear but uncrowded.

ðï¸ June â Yosemite, USA (@ando2chill): Iconic views of El Capitan and Bridalveil Fall at peak flow, without full summer crowds yet.

ð¦ July â Serengeti, Tanzania (@jackswynnerton): Dry season means prime time for the Great Migration and spotting big cats.

ð¼ August â Grand Teton, USA (@maggiizine): Alpine hiking, wildflower meadows, and cool nightsâideal for adventurers and photographers alike.

ð¡ September â Triglav, Slovenia (@youradventureroadtrip): Golden hues light up the Julian Alps, and trails are quieter. Donât miss Lake Bohinj!

ð¦§ October â Tanjung Puting, Borneo (@danielkordan): Best season for spotting wild orangutans along the Sekonyer River via klotok boat.

 ðï¸ November â Torres del Paine, Chile (@timothydhalleine): Spring in Patagonia: snow-capped peaks, active wildlife, and fewer trekkers on the W Trek.

âï¸ December â Urho Kekkonen, Finland (@rikscan & @kira.toussaint): A Lapland dream! Dog sledding, aurora hunting, and endless Arctic landscapes.

Which month would you travel for the views of a lifetime? ðï¸â¨ðï¸

Sukiyaki Mori Kyuu
Japanese Sukiyaki
Set Dinner + Add-ons

Think I have written more than a few posts about this favorite restaurants of mine. Never gets tired of Mori-sanâs sukiyaki which has been a comfort food for me, always get brighten up by his dedication and perfection. 

For newcomers, menu is relatively simple - dinner set at $930 and Omakase starts at $1680. My recommendation would be go for the dinner set and do some seasonal add-ons to your liking. I would always message a few days ahead to check whatâs in season and / or what could be special order from Japan.

On this night, I had the freshest Queen Crab, small one but exceptionally good and it was at exceptional value which I promised not to write the exact price. Also tired squid and sanma which are in season. Specially made uni chawanmushi was a delightful treat. As a beef lover, the beef tenderloin was a must. 

A perfect ten dinner for me. Thank you!

Dinner / October 2023

Just 2 hours by car from Tokyo, I visited a breathtaking cafÃ© overlooking the Kofu Basin and Mount Fuji in Yamanashi!

Many of you might already know this, but itâs located at the summit of the famous âHottarakashi Onsen (campground).â

For those worried about the access, donât be! Itâs a short 5-minute walk up an asphalt slope from the parking lot, so itâs not too tough.

Once you step inside, the stunning view right in front of you leaves you speechlessâ¦

The interior has a mountain lodge vibe, but the lighting and furniture are so stylishâitâs really well-designed!

The house-roasted coffee and cafÃ© lattes were rich and flavorful, and the hojicha latte was also delicious.

I only had coffee this time, but Iâve heard their pizza and pasta are also excellent!

You can hear the sound of the wind rustling through the grass and the evening cicadas chirping.

Without looking at your phone, just sipping coffee while taking in the view makes you want to stay there forever.

Itâs rare to find a place like that, donât you think?

In between our hiking days, we often visit one of the many pretty lakes around, some more remote than othersâºï¸ð»

This lake is called Lago di Braies, and itâs possibly the most popular spot in the Dolomites â we kinda get why, just look at itð¥¹ This is one of those spots that are as pretty in real life as they are in pictures. It can definitely be super busy, but if you choose to be here at sunrise or sunset, we guarantee you a very different experience!

How often to you get off the beaten path when traveling? 

This little gem is called Yixing Suspension Bridge (#ç¾©èåæ©) and is connected to some of the trails around Xiao Wulai Waterfall (#å°çä¾çå¸).

Combine this with a visit to the Atayal Story Park (#æ³°éæäºå¬å) and Luofu Hot Springs (#ç¾æµ®æº«æ³æ¹¯) and youâll have a great day trip on your hands.

Located in Fuxing District of Taoyuan, the area is accessible by public transportation, but probably best experienced by private vehicle or a rental car.

Many overlook this area of Northern Taiwan on their visits, but shouldnât. Amazing cafes, indigenous communities, National Forests, beautiful scenery/trails and tons of history.

dict.value:
Taoyuan Valley Trail (#æ¡æºè°·æ­¥é) is one of the most iconic and picturesque trails in Northern Taiwan (and there are many!). While hiking along the trail isnât too challenge, two out of the three trail entrances require a fair bit of climbing. â¨-â¨Overall, this trail should be rated as easy-to-moderate. At over 14km, this trail can be a full day out or a half-day exploration in the area. Regardless of how you experience it, youâre guaranteed to love it!
-
ð Where is Taoyuan Valley? Just a 1.5-hour drive from Taipei 101, Taoyuan Valley is nestled in Yilan County, offering a serene escape from the cityâs hustle and bustle. ð Take the MRT to Luodong Station and hop on a bus to Daxi (å¤§æºª) in Yilan County. You can also take the train to Yilan and jump off in Dali (å¤§é) or Daxi. â¨-
ð³ Beauty of Taoyuan Valley - This trail is a haven for nature lovers! The rolling meadows and grasslands are the highlight of this trail. But the backdrop of the rugged east coast and Pacific Ocean certainly are the cherries on top.
-
ð» Services & Facilities at Taoyuan Valley - While exploring the trail, youâll find washrooms and water refill stations at key points. The Shiguanyin Temple (ç³è§é³å¯º) and the Tiangong Temple å¤§éå¤©å¬å» in Dali offers a spots to grab water and refreshments. Heck, stop in and learn about the areaâs cultural history while youâre at it. 
 -
ð¥¾ Connecting Trail Adventures - Taoyuan Valley connects to several other trails, making it a hikerâs paradise. Try the Caoling Historic Trail (èå¶ºå¤é) for a historic trek which is part of the famous Tamsui-Kavalan Trails (æ·¡è­å¤é).
-
ð¸ Best Time to Visit - Taoyuan Valley is beautiful year-round, but spring and autumn offer the best and most consistent weather for hiking. In spring, wildflowers bloom, and in autumn, the famous Silvergrass begin to shine! This trail has essentially zero shade, so summer hikes can be a bit overwhelming. Always check the weather forecast before heading out, as this is Taiwan and on the coast, the weather can change quickly
-

Das Luke Knee bzw. eine Variante mit Zwischenstep, kann bei genug Training fast identisch schnell ausgefÃ¼hrt werden, ist aber deutlich energiesparender und hilft dabei kontinuierlich weiterzuarbeiten und damit langfristig schneller zu sein. 

3.Row: Bei Variante eins ist es eine kontinuierlich Zugrate mit geringerem Kraftaufwand beim Ziehen aber einer deutlich hÃ¶heren Frequenz. 
Luke rudert in der selben Pace mit leicht krÃ¤ftigeren ZÃ¼gen, aber einer deutlich geringeren Frequenz und damit einher mehr Pause zwischen den ZÃ¼gen. Beide Varianten kÃ¶nnen fÃ¼r einen funktionieren. Am besten mal selbst ausprobieren, wobei man weniger Energie verbraucht. 

4.Sled Pull: Bei Variante eins wird der Schlitten zum grÃ¶Ãten Teil aus den Armen und dem OberkÃ¶rper gezogen. Bei Variante zwei wird der Schlitten nahezu vollstÃ¤ndig durch das Aufrichten und kleine Schritte nach Hinten gezogen. FÃ¼r den grÃ¶Ãten Teil der Hyroxer wird Variante zwei die energiesparendste AusfÃ¼hrung sein. 

5.SkiErg: Einer der hÃ¤ufigsten Fehler ist nach den ersten 1000m noch hochmotiviert und mit vollem Enthusiasmus ans SkiErg zu rennen und dort mit einer viel zu hohen Pace/ Schlagzahl in die Station zu gehen. Die Zeitersparnis steht meist nicht im VerhÃ¤ltnis zu der Energie, die einem fÃ¼rs weitere Rennen fehlt. 
Deshalb Enthusiasmus runterschrauben und mit einer kontrollierten Schlagzahl/ Pace ans SkiErg gehen.

How we split a 53.26 doubles ð¥

Ski - fast transitions, stay alert and we found 125x4 keeps you fresh but also fast. You can ruin your race here!

Sled Push - half lengths, stops the legs from blowing up early. Fast transitions are crucial here. 

Sled Pull - half lengths makes it faster, but this station can also blow you up quick ð¨

Burpees - 5 reps each is enough to build a rythem but also keep eachother fresh. Bad reps can ruin you here with penaltiesð

Row - rest station, recover well and least transitions as possible is best here. 

Farmers carry - this is where communication becomes super important. Whoever feels better takes the lead here because there is minimal rest between runs. We split it 100 each though ð¤ð¼

Lunges - Enjoy the leg burn, but be smart. The last run and wall balls can ruin a race as well. Change of feel but keep transitions clean and quick. 

Wall Balls - we did 25 each to keep the cadence super fast. We agreed it wasnât worth it holding onto slower reps when we knew we could transition super quick. 

Runs - run your pace from the first run to the last run. So many people ruin there race by running to hard at the start. Your lap paces should be within seconds of eachother not 15 seconds. This is a patient game, respect it! 

Roxzone = full send, donât drag your feet get in and out. 

Most importantly have fun and enjoy the pain ð«¡

Keen to see what @lukepenny14 and I can run fresh and on a indoor course ð

#hyrox #racesplits #raceplan #auckland #hyroxdoubles

FASTER Wall Balls with LESS energy â¡ï¸ð¨ 

SAVE this and try these 4 queues out next time youâre in the gym. 

Speed your HYROX Wall Balls up by making even just some minor changes to your technique.

Here are the common mistakes I see, or ways that make it HARDER ð

1. Bringing your chest down with the ball as you catch up - itâs slower as you can see from this comparison video, and means you have to throw it further 
2. Standing too far from the target - if you do this, you have to throw it forward, and while it seems minor, anything multiplied by 100 will have an impact 
3. Not coming down with the catch - use momentum to get into a rhythm 
4. Holding your breath or not inhaling correctly - Try exhaling as you throw 

Give these a go in training, but it may help you out to either moving faster or more efficiently ð¥
